divine.description: Teleports to nearest Imperial Cult shrine

gondolas.description: Local transport within Vivec City between cantons

cities.name: Balmora
cities.description: District seat of House Hlaalu and the second largest settlement on Vvardenfell after Vivec. The Odai River divides it into four areas: High Town, Commercial District, Labor Town, and nearby Moonmoth Legion Fort.

key_npcs.name: Caius Cosades
key_npcs.role: Grand Spymaster of the Blades, main quest giver

cities.notes: Major trade and quest hub accessible early in gameplay. Starting point for the main quest after arriving from Seyda Neen. Offers essential services, guild opportunities, and substantial quest content.

cities.name: Vivec
cities.description: The largest city on Vvardenfell, located on the southern coast. Comprises nine artificial islands (cantons), each a self-contained district. Holy ground home to the Living God Vivec, patrolled by militant Ordinators. Each canton has four tiers: Underworks (sewers), Canalworks (tombs/storage), Waistworks (shops/residences), and Plaza (manors/fine merchants).
services: Multiple shops and merchants across cantons
cities.notes: Best-connected city in the game with access to all transport types. Maze-like layout can be disorienting; gondolas provide affordable navigation. Strict enforcement by Ordinators. North Landing serves as the main hub with silt strider port and harbor.

cities.name: Ald'ruhn
cities.description: A dusty settlement aligned with House Redoran, located in the Ashlands near Red Mountain. The Manor District is built inside the hollowed shell of an ancient Emperor land-crab known as Skar. Daily ash storms pose a constant threat of blight exposure.
services: Thieves Guild (The Rat In The Pot)
cities.notes: Fighters Guild has a locked room containing nearly complete glass armor. Guard towers hold crates with bonemold armor. Stick to roads and avoid wilderness due to blighted creatures.

cities.name: Sadrith Mora
cities.description: District seat for House Telvanni, meaning 'Forest of the Mushrooms' in Dark Elvish. An island settlement in Zafirbel Bay featuring distinctive architecture of great, magically-formed organic mushrooms arranged around the wizard tower Tel Naga. Accessible only by sea travel and teleportation.

key_npcs.name: Master Neloth
key_npcs.role: Telvanni Council member in Tel Naga tower

key_npcs.name: Angaredhel
key_npcs.role: Prefect of Hospitality at Gateway Inn

cities.notes: Visitors technically need 'Hospitality Papers' from Angaredhel to explore freely, though enforcement is lax. No silt strider service. Only reachable by boat or teleportation.

cities.name: Caldera
cities.description: An Imperial charter town north of Balmora in the West Gash region, built to support nearby ebony mining operations. Features Imperial and Nordic architectural influences arranged in an inverted L-shape between two guard towers.

key_npcs.name: Odral Helvi
key_npcs.role: Governor and House Hlaalu quest giver

key_npcs.name: Stlennius Vibato
key_npcs.role: Mine boss at Caldera Mining Company

key_npcs.name: Creeper
key_npcs.role: Scamp merchant at Ghorak Manor, buys items at full price up to 5000 gold

cities.notes: No silt strider or boat service. Creeper the Scamp is one of the best merchants for selling expensive items. Unguarded alchemy apparatus available in the Mages Guild. Foyada Mamaea to the east provides access to Ghostgate.

cities.name: Seyda Neen
cities.description: A small port village on Vvardenfell's southern Bitter Coast, serving as the 'Gateway to Vvardenfell.' Functions primarily as an entry point for Imperial travelers with a distinctive Grand Pharos lighthouse at the harbor.
services: Arrille's Tradehouse (weapons, armor, spells, potions, scrolls)

key_npcs.name: Socucius Ergalla
key_npcs.role: Chief Census agent; handles character creation

cities.notes: Starting location for the game. Complete 'Report to Caius Cosades' quest before reaching level 4. Nearby smugglers' cave Addamasartus and Samarys Ancestral Tomb (containing the valuable Mentor's Ring). Arrille refuses to trade if player carries Moon Sugar or Skooma.

cities.name: Suran
cities.description: A town on Lake Masobi's eastern coast in the Ascadian Isles, controlled by House Hlaalu. A hub for pilgrims and adventurers traveling northeast toward Molag Amur and Mount Kand. Also a northern base for Hlaalu slavers conducting business with local plantations.
cities.notes: Multiple valuable items available for theft with minimal consequences. Road west to Pelagiad along Lake Masobi. Paths east toward Molag Mar.

cities.name: Pelagiad
cities.description: A farming town in the Ascadian Isles near Lake Amaya's southern shore. Founded after Vvardenfell opened for settlement in 3E 414, features Western architecture resembling a High Rock town, built around Fort Pelagiad.
services: Blacksmith (Shadbak gra-Burbug at fort, Uulernil in town)

key_npcs.name: Ygfa
key_npcs.role: Priestess, sells potions/ingredients/spells, offers Imperial Cult membership

key_npcs.name: Ahnassi
key_npcs.role: Khajiit monk, romance option for male characters

travel_connections: Divine Intervention returns to Fort Pelagiad
cities.notes: No direct fast travel. Convenient waypoint between Seyda Neen, Balmora, and Vivec. Almsivi Intervention splits between Balmora and Vivec depending on position.

cities.name: Gnisis
cities.description: The largest settlement in northwestern Vvardenfell, a frontier town with many comforts for city-dwellers on their first trip into the wilds. Built on a hillside plain beneath a massive egg mine, controlled by House Redoran with Imperial Legion presence at Fort Darius.
services: Tribunal Temple (shrines to St. Llothis and St. Rilm)
Shrine of the Mask (Vivec's Ashmask, disease-curing)
Fort Darius (Imperial Cult and Tribunal shrines)

key_npcs.name: General Darius
key_npcs.role: Imperial Legion commander, sole recruiter for the Legion

key_npcs.name: Hetman Abelmawia
key_npcs.role: Village steward, sells Eggmine Passes and cure blight scrolls

cities.notes: Only place to join the Imperial Legion (via General Darius). Gnisis Eggmine contains accessible Dwemer ruins (Bethamez). Cure Blight Disease spell obtainable through Ashmask shrine quest. Talos Cult secret base in barracks cellar.

cities.name: Ebonheart
cities.description: The seat of Imperial authority in Vvardenfell. A fortified castle town in the Ascadian Isles where the Duke governs alongside his Grand Council.

key_npcs.name: Duke Vedam Dren
key_npcs.role: Rules from Grand Council Chambers, grants stronghold contracts

cities.notes: Underground caves contain the artifact Lord's Mail, accessible via underwater entrance. No silt strider service. Roads connect to Pelagiad and Seyda Neen. Twin Lamps anti-slavery organization present.

cities.name: Hla Oad
cities.description: A small fishing village at the southern end of the Bitter Coast region, southwest of Balmora. Notably sparse with a few desolate fisher huts. A major stopover for smugglers with an underground Camonna Tong base.
services: Fatleg's Drop Off (tavern/general store, run by Trasteve)
Underground Hideout (Camonna Tong base, lockpicks, training)
cities.notes: Minimal legitimate services. Heavy smuggling presence. Almsivi Intervention goes to Balmora, Divine Intervention to Moonmoth Legion Fort. Multiple house faction quests originate here.

cities.name: Maar Gan
cities.description: A walled temple town in the Ashlands north of Ald'ruhn, built by House Redoran using distinctive mud construction and insect shells. The settlement closest to the Ghostfence apart from Ghostgate itself.
services: Tribunal Temple Shrine (priest Salen Ravel, spellmaker, potions)

key_npcs.name: Tashpi Ashibael
key_npcs.role: Healer (involved in Mages Guild necromancy quest)

key_npcs.name: Anhaedra
key_npcs.role: Captive Dremora bound in the shrine

cities.notes: Propylon Index can be found on the shrine offering plate. Bonemold armor in guard tower crates. Divine Intervention goes to Buckmoth Legion Fort, Almsivi Intervention to Ald'ruhn. Ground routes lead to Urshilaku Camp.

cities.name: Molag Mar
cities.description: A modest town in the Molag Amur region designed as a waypoint for pilgrims. Resembles a single canton of Vivec set down between regions, with an open-air design unlike the domed cantons of Vivec.
cities.notes: Serves primarily as a resting point for travelers and pilgrims. Two major pilgrimage sites nearby: Mount Kand and Mount Assarnibibi, accessible via foyada (ancient lava tubes). Almsivi Intervention works here; Divine Intervention leads to Wolverine Hall.

cities.name: Tel Mora
cities.description: The northernmost Telvanni-held island, home to Mistress Dratha, one of the oldest Telvanni wizard lords. Features mushroom dwellings surrounding a distinctive tower between two islands with connecting walkways, in the Azura's Coast region.
services: Spell Vendors (Diren Vendu, Salama Andrethi, Tinaso Alan)
cities.notes: The entire settlement operates under Dratha's female-only preferences. Male characters may shop but should avoid the tower itself. Female players receive warmer welcome and tower access. No silt strider or guild guide service.
